steps.title: Use the one-solution condition
steps.content: The line $y=-1.5$ and the parabola $y=x^2+8x+a$ have exactly one distinct real solution only when the line touches the parabola at its vertex.

steps.title: Find the vertex height
steps.content: Complete the square: $x^2+8x+a=(x+4)^2+a-16$. The vertex has $y$-coordinate $a-16$.

steps.title: Set the vertex on the line
